common_assumptions: File paths and directory structure remain stable between tool calls within the same chain
Type-checker passing (tsc --noEmit) implies feature correctness, not just syntactic validity
Shell tool availability (pm2, gh, rg) without prior which/version check
Prior process or environment state is unchanged since the last read
Single-file edits resolve issues without cascading effects in sibling files
strength_patterns: Consistent read-before-edit discipline: targeted file reads precede almost every modification across chains
Verification loops using type-checkers and build outputs (tsc, svelte-check, npm run build) after editing batches
Multi-source debugging: config files, logs, environment probes, and live process output checked in concert
weakness_patterns: File-level fixation: statusline.sh and +page.svelte each appear 30+ times in single chains without a context-broadening pause
Symptom-driven iteration: long debugging chains (110+ steps) retry variants of the same fix rather than stepping back to reframe the root cause
Near-zero alternative breadth: chains almost universally commit to one approach before executing, with no visible branch evaluation
